With a specialty in infotainment brochures, [[Hologram/Legends|holopromotions]], and other media, the company '''Galaxy Publishing''' had produced material of this nature for [[Galaxy Tours/Legends|Galaxy Tours]], a tourism and [[Passenger liner/Legends|starline]] operator, and [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]], a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] manufacturer. Despite primarily being a publishing firm, the company for a [[Time/Legends|time]] served as the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Species/Legends|species]]' public relations firm, representing several of their businesses. This engagement was not a protracted agreement as the company's board of directors realized the great difficulty in improving the image of the Hutts.<ref name="HSCS" />

Galaxy Publishing was also a [[contributing sponsor]] of the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] as a result of providing the equivalent of fifty trillion [[Credit chip/Legends|credits]] in equipment, funds, and services. As a contributing sponsor, they received a proportional share of [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] profits as well as nominal discounts on bulk goods and raw materials, though they did not obtain the increased benefits of being selected as a [[voting sponsor]].<ref name="HSCS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Galaxy Publishing was mentioned in ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'' for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', written by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and published in [[1993]].<ref name="HSCS" />
